title: Case: Adjourned Iturgen Bolir v. Gaven Carnahan (2026) CV 01

posts.author: jotoho
posts.rawText:
Iturgen Bolir (jotoho), Plaintiff

v.

Gaven Carnahan (Rollergang), Defendant

Civil Complaint:

This civil complaint is intended to recover funds I consider to have been stolen by the Defendant.

Parties:

Plaintiff: jotoho
Defendant: Rollergang

Factual Allegations:

On 2026-01-04, the Defendant repeatedly stepped onto farm plot f022 without permission by jumping/glitching over the fence surrounding the property, currently rented from the Government by the Plaintiff.
While on f022 or in close proximity, the Defendant repeatedly murdered and stole from the Plaintiff who was on plot f022, with the stolen funds adding up to a total of $516.66

Legal Claims:

That the Defendant did not have a legal claim to the money stolen from the Plaintiff
That the Plaintiff has a legal right to the return of those funds stolen from the Plaintiff by the Defendant
An excerpt of the client log (the full file was too large for this forum to accept) and screenshots of #global-feed are attached to this civil complaint, in order to prove that the described battle(s) occurred and that the described amount of money was stolen by the Defendant.

Prayer for Relief:

The Plaintiff respectfully requests that this Court grant the following Prayer for Relief:

Compensatory Damages for the funds taken by the Defendant in the sum of $516.66
If any court fees arise for the Plaintiff as a consequence of this trial, Special Damages in the sum of those fees.
Any other relief this court deems just and proper.

The Plaintiff generously refrains from requesting compensation for the loss of about 40 levels of XP, as a result of the first murder against him, as well as any potentially due compensation for the cost of gear durability and ammunition consumed as part of the repeated attacks on his person and the Plaintiff's necessary self-defense.

Verification:

I, jotoho, hereby affirm that the allegations in the complaint AND all subsequent statements made in court are true and correct to the best of the plaintiff's knowledge, information, and belief and that any falsehoods may bring the penalty of perjury.

Let it be noted, that I will represent myself for the duration of this case, and will personally notify this court in case I unexpectedly need to entrust it to another Lawyer.

posts.author: jotoho
posts.rawText:
I request to append this screenshot to the evidence.

posts.author: Westray
posts.rawText:
Writ of Summons

Azalea Isles District Court, Civil Case (CV)

Case No. CV-26-01

Plaintiff: Iturgen Bolir (jotoho)
Defendant: Gaven Carnahan (Rollergang)

The Defendant is required to appear before the court in the case of Iturgen Bolir v. Gaven Carnahan. Failure to respond within 48 hours may result in a default judgement. Both parties are ask to familiarize themselves with the relevant court documents, including proper formats, as well as the laws referenced in the complaint. Ensure that you comply with any court orders.

If you wish to hold this trial at the Azalea Courthouse in-person, please note that in your response. The Court will try to work with both parties to hold live hearings at convenient times.

Signed,
Hon. Chief Justice Raymond West

posts.author: jotoho
posts.rawText:
Motion for Default Judgment

As the Defendant appears to not have responded to the Writ of Summons in the more than 48 hours since Your Honor's Writ of Summons, I request that this court move towards a Default Judgment.

posts.author: Westray
posts.rawText:
Before the Court issues its verdict, I would like to ask that the Plaintiff clarify what they are asking for from the Court with respect to the last two points of their Prayer for Relief:

jotoho said:
Compensatory Damages for the funds taken by the Defendant in the sum of $516.66
If any court fees arise for the Plaintiff as a consequence of this trial, Special Damages in the sum of those fees.
Any other relief this court deems just and proper.

Is the Plaintiff seeking more than $516.66 in compensation from the Defendant at this time?

posts.author: jotoho
posts.rawText:
Westray said:
Before the Court issues its verdict, I would like to ask that the Plaintiff clarify what they are asking for from the Court with respect to the last two points of their Prayer for Relief:

Is the Plaintiff seeking more than $516.66 in compensation from the Defendant at this time?

As this court seems to not have charged any fees thus far, the Plaintiff requests to drop points 2 and 3 from the Prayer for Relief, Your Honor.

The Plaintiff currently only seeks the Compensatory Damages in point 1.

posts.author: Westray
posts.rawText:
Default Judgement
Azalea Isles District Court, Civil Case (CV)

Case No. CV-26-01
Iturgen Bolir v. Gaven Carnahan

The Court is prepared to accept the motion to default judgement as a result of the Defendant's lack of response. Based on the current facts presented, the Court hereby rules in favour of the Plaintiff, granting the following relief:

1. The Defendant, Gaven Carnahan (Rollergang), is hereby ordered to provide $516.66 in compensatory damages to the Plaintiff.

In the event that the Defendant is unable to pay these funds to the Plaintiff themselves, the Plaintiff may use this ruling to ask that the government facilitate such transaction.

Be advised that a default judgement is entered based on only the facts presented in the case filing given the Defendant's inability to respond well beyond the specified deadlines. The Court is hereby adjourned.

Signed,
Hon. Chief Justice Raymond West
